learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate ethical and professional decision making and behaviour appropriate to exercise physiology while applying knowledge and understanding of standards of clinical practice, relevant codes of conduct, and client rights.
Demonstrate a competent level of effective verbal, non-verbal and written communication skills applied to all aspects of practice including interactions with clients, professionals and the community.

Demonstrate a competent ability to appropriately, safely, accurately, systematically and efficiently assess a client to determine and prioritise problems via gathering, integrating and interpreting appropriate data related to the patient.
Demonstrate an appropriate process of clinical reasoning in making decisions related to the client such as developing a hypothesis and determining patient outcomes.
Demonstrate an understanding of the importance of goal-setting with clients and self-management for return of function, work and leisure activities.

Select, develop and implement safe, appropriate and effective exercise physiology interventions to a competent standard using problem solving skills such as critical thinking and judgement while aiming to achieve client treatment goals.
Demonstrate the ability to provide advice and education relevant to client goals, health status and individual circumstances.
Demonstrate the integration of evidence-based practice into the practicum setting.

Demonstrate an appreciation of the scope of practice of an exercise physiologist as well as the roles of other disciplines and participate as a member of an interdisciplinary / multi-professional team.
Demonstrate an ability to reflect on one's own professional development and clinical exercise physiology practice.

assessments.due_date: 5:00 pm on the Friday of your third week of placement.
assessments.task_description: To meet ESSA Accredited Exercise Physiologist (AEP) Practicum Standards, students must maintain a logbook documenting all practicum hours completed during placement. A minimum of 180 hours for EXMD4701 is required across a range of clinical exercise physiology activities to demonstrate competency in assessment, exercise prescription, and delivery within the AEP scope of practice. The initial logbook submission is used to check that your practicum hours are being recorded in line with logbook reporting requirements.
assessments.submission_guidelines: For this assessment, you must complete your first three weeks of placement logbook entries using the provided logbook template. Once completed, upload your logbook template to ePortfolio using the submission link available on Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date: 5:00 pm, 14 calendar days after your placement completion date
assessments.task_description: The final logbook submission requires you to submit your completed logbook, with all practicum hours recorded in accordance with the relevant logbook reporting requirements.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date: 5:00 pm on the Friday of your third week of placement.
assessments.task_description:
As student practitioners on practicum, you are required to critically reflect on your performance and self-assess your developing capabilities. This self-assessment supports your

development as a reflective practitioner, a key capability embedded within the ESSA Professional Practice Standards. You will assess yourself against the ESSA Professional

Standards based on your experiences from the first half of your placement.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Click the submission link on Blackboard to access the self-reflection form in ePortfolio. Complete all required sections of the form, including the written reflection responses and
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date: 5:00 pm on the Friday of your third week of placement.
assessments.task_description:
As student practitioners on practicum, you are required to receive structured feedback on your developing professional capabilities. At the mid-point of your placement, your

supervisor will assess your performance against the ESSA Professional Standards and provide ratings and commentary based on your progress to date. The purpose of this

assessment is to support your development as a reflective practitioner, guide professional growth, and identify strengths and areas requiring further improvement during the

remainder of your placement.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Students are responsible for generating the correct assessment link in ePortfolio and sending it to their supervisor. Students must also organise a suitable time to meet with theirsupervisor to discuss and complete the assessment. Detailed instructions for generating and sharing the link are provided on Blackboard. The supervisor is responsible forcompleting and submitting the assessment form in ePortfolio.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date: 5:00 pm, 14 calendar days after your placement completion date
assessments.task_description: At the end of your placement, your supervisor will conduct a Final Professional Attributes Assessment to evaluate your overall performance and readiness for professional practice. This assessment uses the same four-point scale applied throughout your placement. This final assessment provides an opportunity for you and your supervisor to review your performance, acknowledge strengths, identify any remaining areas for development, and confirm that you have met the minimum professional competency required for successful course completion. Unlike the Professional Attributes Intermediate Assessment, the Professional Attributes Final Assessment is summative and contributes to your overall course result. To pass the placement, you must meet or exceed expectations in all Standards.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Students are responsible for generating the correct assessment link in ePortfolio and sending it to their supervisor to complete. Students must also organise a suitable time to meet with their supervisor to discuss the assessment. Detailed instructions for generating and sharing the link are provided on Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date: 5:00 pm, 14 calendar days after your placement completion date
assessments.task_description: To demonstrate that you have met the ESSA Practicum Standards during your placement, you are required to complete and submit a Record of Student Engagement Form. The Record of Student Engagement provides a structured summary of the activities you undertook during placement, drawing on evidence contained in your practicum logbooks, and a reflective account of how your placement contributed to the development of the ESSA professional attributes required for accreditation. The Record of Student Engagement must be completed using the ESSA-approved template and must be reviewed and signed by your practicum supervisor.
assessments.submission_guidelines: For this assessment, you must complete the ROSE form using the provided template and submit it to your supervisor for review and signature. Once completed, upload your
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
